source: Incus Server - 9 Snapshot Server: Mirroring the server and establishing trust between the two

questions.q: How closely should the snapshot server match the primary, and how do you build it?
options: It must mirror the primary in every way -- repeat chapters 1-4 on it
It can be any spare machine with no setup
It must run a different OS
questions.why: The snapshot server has to mirror production, so you redo the install/ZFS/init/firewall chapters on it.

questions.q: Without a DNS server (e.g. in a lab), how do the two servers resolve each other's names?
options: Add both servers' IPs and names to /etc/hosts on each machine
questions.why: Add entries like '192.168.1.106 incus-primary' to /etc/hosts on both.

questions.q: How do you let the two servers exchange traffic through firewalld?
options: Add the other server's IP as a source in the trusted zone (firewall-cmd zone=trusted add-source=<ip> --permanent), then reload
questions.why: Each server trusts the other's IP via the trusted zone.

questions.q: Establish the trust relationship from incus-primary to the snapshot server (named incus-snapshot).
questions.why: incus remote add shows a cert to accept, then prompts for the trust password set during init.

questions.q: Which password does `incus remote add` prompt for?
options: The trust password you set during incus admin init
questions.why: It is the init-time trust password (keep these saved securely).

questions.q: Before migrating snapshots, what profile step is needed on the snapshot server?
options: Create (or copy over) the macvlan profile, e.g. incus profile copy macvlan incus-snapshot
Recreate the ZFS pool as btrfs
questions.why: Profiles used on the primary (macvlan) must exist on the snapshot server; you can copy it over if the parent interface names match.
